item.title: Hawley Primary School
item.dc:creator: Christopher Morrall
item.description:
Keir spent the afternoon with pupils and staff at Hawley Primary School to celebrate their successes, including a new free Best Start Breakfast Club, and listen to their priorities for education in Holborn & St Pancras.

The post Hawley Primary School appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
Keir spent the afternoon with pupils and staff at Hawley Primary School to celebrate their successes, including a new free Best Start Breakfast Club, and listen to their priorities for education in Holborn & St Pancras.

The post Hawley Primary School appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: Coram’s Fields Youth Centre
item.dc:creator: Christopher Morrall
item.description:
Keir met with staff and young people at Coram Field’s Youth Centre, to listen to their perspectives on the challenges young people face growing up in Holborn & St Pancras, and discuss the Labour Government’s new National Youth Strategy.

The post Coram’s Fields Youth Centre appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
Keir met with staff and young people at Coram Field’s Youth Centre, to listen to their perspectives on the challenges young people face growing up in Holborn & St Pancras, and discuss the Labour Government’s new National Youth Strategy.

The post Coram’s Fields Youth Centre appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: Community Conversation
item.dc:creator: Christopher Morrall
item.description:
Keir joined residents and community organisations from St Pancras & Somers Town, King’s Cross and Regent’s Park wards over tea and biscuits. They shared their local priorities and discussed ways in which the government is empowering communities and tackling the cost of living.

The post Community Conversation appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
Keir joined residents and community organisations from St Pancras & Somers Town, King’s Cross and Regent’s Park wards over tea and biscuits. They shared their local priorities and discussed ways in which the government is empowering communities and tackling the cost of living.

The post Community Conversation appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: Camden Council Inclusive Growth Breakfast
item.dc:creator: Christopher Morrall
item.description:
Keir met with Camden apprentices, and joined key businesses and institutions to discuss ways in which they can work in partnership with Camden Council to achieve inclusive growth, tackle inequality, and benefit their organisations.

The post Camden Council Inclusive Growth Breakfast appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
Keir met with Camden apprentices, and joined key businesses and institutions to discuss ways in which they can work in partnership with Camden Council to achieve inclusive growth, tackle inequality, and benefit their organisations.

The post Camden Council Inclusive Growth Breakfast appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: 20th anniversary of the 7/7 London bombings
item.dc:creator: Conor Pope
item.description:
On the 20th anniversary of the 7/7 London bombings, Keir stood with survivors, loved ones and first responders at the Tavistock Square Memorial in the constituency, to remember and pay tribute to the 13 victims and survivors of the attack in front of the British Medical Association offices, and to pay tribute to the emergency services and members of the […]

The post 20th anniversary of the 7/7 London bombings appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
On the 20th anniversary of the 7/7 London bombings, Keir stood with survivors, loved ones and first responders at the Tavistock Square Memorial in the constituency, to remember and pay tribute to the 13 victims and survivors of the attack in front of the British Medical Association offices, and to pay tribute to the emergency services and members of the public who helped in the aftermath.

The post 20th anniversary of the 7/7 London bombings appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: We Make Camden Summit 2025
item.dc:creator: Conor Pope
item.description:
In May, Keir joined over 200 Camden residents, organisations, and businesses at the British Library’s Knowledge Centre for Camden Council’s fourth annual We Make Camden Summit. Through collaboration and innovative mission-led projects, Camden’s brilliant community works together to create meaningful change across the borough.

The post We Make Camden Summit 2025 appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
In May, Keir joined over 200 Camden residents, organisations, and businesses at the British Library’s Knowledge Centre for Camden Council’s fourth annual We Make Camden Summit. Through collaboration and innovative mission-led projects, Camden’s brilliant community works together to create meaningful change across the borough.

The post We Make Camden Summit 2025 appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: London AI Campus
item.dc:creator: Conor Pope
item.description:
When Keir first welcomed Google to the constituency, he challenged them to help break down the barriers between big business and our young people, and they did just that!  It was with great pleasure that he joined the launch of Google’s first AI Campus, in heart of Somers’ Town, offering opportunities for Camden’s future AI […]

The post London AI Campus appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
When Keir first welcomed Google to the constituency, he challenged them to help break down the barriers between big business and our young people, and they did just that!  It was with great pleasure that he joined the launch of Google’s first AI Campus, in heart of Somers’ Town, offering opportunities for Camden’s future AI Leaders.

The post London AI Campus appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: Camden Anchors
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Moorish
item.description:
Keir was delighted to attend the second meeting of the Camden Anchors Forum. Alongside Tulip Siddiq and Cllr Georgia Gould, they discussed how to work together to deliver opportunities for local people and reduce inequality, with a particular focus on apprenticeships and young people.

The post Camden Anchors appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
Keir was delighted to attend the second meeting of the Camden Anchors Forum. Alongside Tulip Siddiq and Cllr Georgia Gould, they discussed how to work together to deliver opportunities for local people and reduce inequality, with a particular focus on apprenticeships and young people.

The post Camden Anchors appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: International Women’s Day
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Moorish
item.description:
On International Women’s Day, Keir was proud to support the FA’s Let Girls Play Initiative and join young footballers (and Arsenal fans) with Fara Williams at Haverstock School. Labour will close the gender play gap so girls have equal access to sport at school.

The post International Women’s Day appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
On International Women’s Day, Keir was proud to support the FA’s Let Girls Play Initiative and join young footballers (and Arsenal fans) with Fara Williams at Haverstock School. Labour will close the gender play gap so girls have equal access to sport at school.

The post International Women’s Day appeared first on Keir Starmer.

item.title: Maitland Park
item.dc:creator: Conor Pope
item.description:
When opening new homes on the Maitland Park Keir said: “Well done to every single person who has been involved in this project. To walk in and see the buildings this morning was fantastic. And it was great to meet some of the residents who’ve recently moved in. “This isn’t just about some bricks and […]

The post Maitland Park appeared first on Keir Starmer.
item.content:encoded:
When opening new homes on the Maitland Park Keir said:

“Well done to every single person who has been involved in this project. To walk in and see the buildings this morning was fantastic. And it was great to meet some of the residents who’ve recently moved in.

“This isn’t just about some bricks and some windows and a roof; this is about life chances, aspiration and equal opportunity for everyone. When I was growing up we didn’t have a lot of money, but we did have a house. And that gave me a security to go on and do some of the things that I’ve done in my life. I want every child to have that chance.

“This is what good councils can do. I’m very proud of Camden Council and what it has achieved here.  I want to see a similar thing across the country. I want all councils to have this level of ambition.

“Labour’s mission is to build many, many more affordable homes.. It will be one of the driving purposes of my government to  ensure that across the country everybody gets the chance to live in accommodation as good as this accommodation here.”

The post Maitland Park appeared first on Keir Starmer.
